title_en: Why Old Master paintings are back in vogue
title_zh: 为什么古典大师作品又回归流行
rubric_en: Attitudes towards art, value and what it takes to be a good collector are changing
rubric_zh: 人们对艺术、价值观和“好收藏家”标准的认知正在发生变化

paragraphs.en: ON FIRST GLANCE the painting—by an unknown Dutch artist in the 17th century—seemed unlikely to spark a bidding war. It depicts two skulls and a handwritten note asking “Uter dominus, uter servus?” (Which is the master, which is the slave?) Yet on June 30th heady bidding at an auction at Christie’s in London pushed the painting’s price to £431,000 ($575,000), about four times the estimate. There is a “yearning for the unusual”, explains Andrew Fletcher, head of Christie’s Old Masters department, who has seen a surge of interest in “the weird and wonderful”.
paragraphs.zh: 乍一看，这幅画——出自17世纪一位身份不明的荷兰画家——似乎不太可能引发竞拍大战。画面上有两个骷髅，旁边还写着“Uter dominus, uter servus？”（谁是主人，谁是奴隶？）这样的手写问题。然而，6月30日伦敦佳士得（Christie’s）拍卖会上，拍卖氛围在升温，最终将这幅画的价格推高到43.1万英镑（约57.5万美元），约为估价的四倍。佳士得古代艺术部负责人安德鲁·弗莱彻（Andrew Fletcher）解释说，这是“对异类和奇特之物的渴望”，他近来观察到“怪异而精彩”的作品受到更大关注。

paragraphs.en: Old Masters—a centuries-spanning category that includes works typically completed before 1850—have seen new life breathed into them. In that same Christie’s auction records were set for six artists, including Sir Thomas Lawrence, an English painter, whose portrait of the Duke of Wellington (pictured above), the general who beat Napoleon, fetched £9.7m. “It’s the most optimistic moment I can remember in the Old Masters market for some years,” says Mr Fletcher.
paragraphs.zh: “古代大师”（Old Masters）——通常指完成于1850年前的作品，跨越几个世纪——正在迎来新生。在同一场佳士得拍卖会上，六位艺术家的作品都刷新了纪录，其中包括英国画家托马斯·劳伦斯爵士（Sir Thomas Lawrence）的作品——这位画的是威灵顿公爵（上图），这位战胜拿破仑的将军，其作品以970万英镑成交。“这是我这些年来在古代大师市场上记忆中最乐观的一刻，”劳伦斯先生说。

paragraphs.en: The value of Old Masters sales worldwide in 2025 was $1.2bn, 30% higher than a year earlier. But data may not capture the “full story” of the uptick, as many paintings are sold privately, says Patrick Williams, a gallerist in New York. Younger buyers are showing more enthusiasm. This year around 16% of bidders in Old Masters sales at Sotheby’s, another auction house, are under the age of 40, nearly triple the share from five years ago.
paragraphs.zh: 2025年全球古代大师作品的销售额为12亿美元，比上一年高出30%。但正如纽约的画廊商帕特里克·威廉姆斯（Patrick Williams）所说，数据可能并未捕捉到增长的“全貌”，因为许多画作是通过私人渠道售出的。更年轻的买家正表现出更大热情。今年，在苏富比（Sotheby’s）等另一家拍卖行的古代大师拍卖中，竞拍者中40岁以下人群约占16%，几乎是五年前比例的三倍。

paragraphs.en: Until the 1980s Old Masters ruled the art world. But then collectors decided the category was too old-timey and turned to collecting Impressionist and contemporary works instead. Several things have changed in the past two years. First, some parts of the market for contemporary art showed signs of volatility. Collectors realised that Old Masters are stable and significantly less expensive. “For $250,000 you can buy a painting that could hang in the Louvre, but $10m gets you a bad Picasso,” says Mr Williams. There is also scarcity: more Old Master paintings are entering museum collections every year.
paragraphs.zh: 直到20世纪80年代，古代大师作品一直主导着艺术界。但后来，收藏家们认为这一类别过于“过时”，转而去收藏印象派和当代艺术作品。过去两年，情况发生了几项变化。首先，当代艺术市场的某些板块出现了波动迹象。收藏家意识到，古代大师作品更稳定，价格也明显更低。威廉姆斯先生说：“两十五万美元就能买到一幅能挂在卢浮宫的画，但要找一幅像样的毕加索却要一千万美元。”另外，稀缺性也在起作用：每年都有越来越多古代大师作品进入博物馆收藏。

paragraphs.en: Art buyers have also become more open-minded about mixing pictures from different periods. A “turning-point” came in 2017, says Sylvie Winckler, a Belgian collector: that was when Christie’s sold Leonardo da Vinci’s heavily restored “Salvator Mundi” for $450m, the highest price ever paid for an artwork, in a post-war- and contemporary-art auction. It showed how older art could look sleek alongside more modern works in the white boxes many wealthy people call homes.
paragraphs.zh: 艺术买家也变得更愿意把不同时期的作品放在一起收藏。比利时收藏家 Sylvie Winckler 表示，2017 年出现了一个“转折点”：那一年，佳士得在一场兼售战后艺术与当代艺术的拍卖会上，以 4.5 亿美元拍出了达·芬奇那幅经过大幅修复的《救世主》（Salvator Mundi），这是当时有史以来艺术品成交的最高价。这说明，即使是古老艺术，也能与更现代的作品并列，放在许多有钱人所谓的“白盒子式”住宅空间里看起来同样时髦。

paragraphs.en: Opinions about what constitutes “good” collecting have changed, too. Conventionally, buyers of Old Masters were like PhD candidates, specialising in areas such as 18th-century French still-lifes. Today people buy art more eclectically and expansively. A new generation of collectors is driven “less by traditional connoisseurship and more by image and the visible impact of the work itself”, says Valentina Castellani, author of “Trading Beauty”, a history of art buying.
paragraphs.zh: 对于“什么才算好的收藏”这一问题的看法也在变化。过去，古代大师作品的买家往往像博士生一样，专注于某一细分领域，比如18世纪法国静物画。今天，人们购买艺术的方式更杂、范围更广。一群新一代藏家更“少受传统鉴赏家观念驱动”，而是“更多被作品本身的视觉效果和可见冲击力吸引”，这是《Trading Beauty》作者、艺术购买史研究者瓦伦蒂娜·卡斯特拉尼（Valentina Castellani）的看法。

paragraphs.en: This means that portraits and figurative art are in vogue, because they are Instagrammable. People spend so much time looking at pictures of other people on social media that they are primed to connect to painted ones. There is an allure to old-fashioned fabrics and romanticising of what appears to be a simpler life (if you ignore the revolutions, plagues and awful dentistry of past eras). Charles Stewart, the boss of Sotheby’s, attributes resurging interest in Old Masters to an “AI effect”: “Older things feel more artisanal and human,” he says.
paragraphs.zh: 这意味着肖像画和具象艺术重新流行，因为它们更“适合在 Instagram 展示”。人们在社交媒体上看着他人照片花了太多时间，因而更容易对绘画中的人物产生情感连接。古朴的服饰、以及对看似更单纯生活方式的浪漫化都很有吸引力（如果把过去时代的革命、瘟疫和糟糕的牙医技术先放在一边）。苏富比（Sotheby’s）负责人查尔斯·斯图尔特（Charles Stewart）将古代大师作品复苏的热潮归因于“AI 效应”：“老旧的事物听起来更手工、更有人情味”，他说。

paragraphs.en: Dealers have become more masterful in marketing to a new clientele. Secular images are easier to sell than religious ones, as are works with an intriguing back story. This was evident at TEFAF Maastricht, an art fair that is the biggest gathering for Old Masters buyers and dealers, in March. Paul Smeets, a gallerist, displayed a painting by Guido Cagnacci, an Italian who died in 1663. It was of the Virgin Mary, adorned with a golden halo, reading a book. “Ten years ago we would have just left ‘Virgin Reading’ as a title,” says Mr Smeets. But instead he wanted to highlight that the sitter might have been the paramour of the artist. So he called it “Possibly a portrait of Maddalena Fontanafredda as the Virgin Reading”. Clunky, yes, but it added drama and passion to a familiar scene. The work sold quickly. Old Masters have a lot of lovers these days. ■
paragraphs.zh: 画廊经营者在向新买家群体推销时，也变得更善于“包装”作品。世俗题材的作品比宗教题材更容易销售，故事背景越有趣也越有吸引力。这一点在马斯特里赫特的 TEFAF 艺博会（The European Fine Art Fair）得到了体现——这是3月古代大师买家和经销商规模最大的聚会。画廊商 Paul Smeets 展出了一幅意大利画家 Guido Cagnacci（1663年去世）的作品，画的是戴着金色光环、正在看书的圣母玛利亚。他说：“十年前我们可能只会把它叫作‘Virgin Reading’。”但他改成了另一个标题，以突出这位模特儿可能是画家的情妇：“Possibly a portrait of Maddalena Fontanafredda as the Virgin Reading”。这个标题很笨拙，的确，但它让一个熟悉的场景更有戏剧性和情感张力。这幅画很快就卖出。如今，古代大师作品有很多“情人”。 ■

paragraphs.zh: 如要了解更多最新书籍、电影、电视剧、专辑和争议内容，请订阅《Plot Twist》——我们每周面向订阅用户推出的电子通讯。
